evidence: The paper uses Random Forest with TLPO-CV-RFE to identify "most important features" (C4-carnitine, lysoPC a C28:1), interpreting their ranking and importance. However, multiple independent methods validate these findings: "Random Forest and Support Vector Machine analysis...identified lysoPC a C28:1 and C28:0 [with SVM]" showing two algorithms agree; ANOVA independently confirmed these metabolites differed significantly (p<0.05 after FDR); cross-validation on held-out test pairs; external validation in Ferrario et al. dataset; kinetic modeling validated pathway involvement. The conclusions center on metabolic dysregulation mechanisms and specific biomarker identification rather than on impurity importance magnitude claims.
